text: Last summer, I finally took a trip to Japan that I had been planning for months. From the moment I arrived, everything felt new and exciting. The food was amazing—I tried sushi, ramen, and sweet mochi that melted in my mouth. However, there were some language barriers; ordering food in a small restaurant was challenging, but using hand gestures and a smile helped. Walking through busy streets and quiet temples, I learned so much about Japanese traditions and politeness. Now, looking back, the memories of that journey warm my heart. Traveling taught me that stepping out of your comfort zone is worth it—you discover not only new cultures, but also a little more about yourself.
